text.tspan: Problem: every LLM app re-implemented its own tool-integration layer.
Each (app, tool) pair was bespoke. Tools couldn't move across apps.
Nov 2024: Anthropic launches MCP - 'USB-C for AI tools.'
2025: adopted by OpenAI, Google, Microsoft - now universal.
CLIENT (an LLM app): Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code,
OpenAI Agents, custom apps...
| JSON-RPC over stdio / HTTP / SSE
SERVER (a tool provider): filesystem, GitHub, Slack, Notion,
Postgres, custom internal tools...
Many clients can connect to many servers.
One server can serve many clients.
RESOURCES: read-only data (files, DB rows, API responses).
Client can list / read; updates push to subscribers.
TOOLS: callable functions with typed args.
The LLM picks one to call; server executes.
PROMPTS: named, parameterized prompt templates the server
exposes for the client to use.
Nov 25, 2024: Anthropic launches MCP open spec
Dec 2024: Claude Desktop ships with MCP support
Q1 2025: Cursor, Continue, Zed adopt
Mar 26, 2025: OpenAI announces MCP adoption (Agents SDK)
Apr 2025: Google announces MCP support (Gemini)
May 2025: Microsoft commits MCP (Copilot Studio)
2025-2026: hundreds of public MCP servers
(filesystem, GitHub, Slack, Notion, Linear,
Postgres, Brave Search, Puppeteer, etc.)
